description:
REQ-030 Phase 4, items 9-11: Add time-based job scheduling to the daemon beyond the fixed interval_secs tick.

Changes needed:
1. Add to DaemonProjectConfig in daemon_config.rs:
   ```
   cron_jobs: Vec<CronJobConfig>
   ```
   Where CronJobConfig has:
   - `id: String` (unique job identifier)
   - `schedule: String` (support simple specs first: "@hourly", "@daily", "@weekly", "HH:MM" for daily at time)
   - `pipeline_id: Option<String>` (workflow pipeline to run)
   - `task_filter: Option<CronTaskFilter>` with tag/status/priority filters
   - `action: CronAction` enum: RunWorkflow, CreateTask, RunCommand(String)
   - `enabled: bool` (default true)
2. Add cron state file `.ao/state/daemon-cron-state.json` storing `{ job_id: last_run_at }` timestamps
3. On each tick, evaluate which cron jobs are due:
   - Parse schedule into next-run time
   - Compare against last_run_at from state file
   - If due: execute action, update last_run_at
4. Add `ao daemon cron list` — show configured jobs with last run time and next due time
5. Add `ao daemon cron fire <job-id>` — manually trigger a cron job
6. Add `ao daemon config set --cron-job '{"id":"morning-review","schedule":"09:00","pipeline_id":"code-review","enabled":true}'`
7. Respect active_hours gating: skip cron jobs outside active hours (if configured)
8. Add tests: job fires when due, doesn't re-fire before interval, manual fire works, disabled jobs skip

Start with simple time specs (@hourly, @daily, HH:MM). Full cron expression parsing (e.g., `0 9 * * MON-FRI`) can be added later via the `cron` crate.

Key files:
- crates/orchestrator-core/src/daemon_config.rs (CronJobConfig, DaemonProjectConfig)
- crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/runtime/runtime_daemon/daemon_scheduler_project_tick.rs
- New file: daemon_cron.rs (cron evaluation and state management)
title: Add cron job support to daemon config and tick loop
